Oil Prices Spike as Iran Declares Strait of Hormuz Closed
headlines.summary: Oil prices surged again in early Asian trade on Thursday after Iran declared the Strait of Hormuz closed following a fresh round of U.S. strikes on the country. At the time of writing, Brent crude had climbed 2.26% to trade at $95.20 per barrel, while West Texas Intermediate had

headlines.title: How Fake News Became the Most Dangerous Force in Energy Markets
headlines.summary: We live in a dangerously synthetic world. Scalper-style traders reportedly generated significant profits on suspiciously timed oil trades surrounding Iran war developments in Q2. AI-generated content is creating new verification challenges. Fake news about an attack on Saudi oil

headlines.title: Traders Are Shorting Oil As If The Hormuz Crisis Is Over
headlines.summary: In yet another sign that the paper oil market may be too complacent about the magnitude of the supply disruption in the Middle East, trades have been boosting their short positions in oil futures for most of the past two months. headlines.title: Oil Prices Are Up. Investment Isn't Following
headlines.summary: When the International Energy Agency issued its 2026 outlook on energy investment, it had a surprise for its audience, predicting a decline in oil investment, instead of an increase, as the current price environment would suggest. headlines.title: Germany's Big LNG Deal With Canada May Never Deliver a Single Cargo
headlines.summary: The Iran war has made supplies of liquefied natural gas, or LNG, the most strategic since Russia’s invasion of Ukraine in 2022. headlines.title: Energy Prices Drive U.S. Inflation To Three-Year High
headlines.summary: U.S. inflation climbed above 4% for the first time in three years in May as rising energy costs tied to the Iran war pushed consumer costs sharply higher, with the Consumer Price Index rising 0.5% from April and 4.2% from a year earlier, according to data released Wednesday by th

headlines.title: Hormuz Shutdown Forces Malaysia to Overhaul Crude Supply Chain
headlines.summary: Malaysia is looking to diversify its crude sourcing with supply from the U.S., South America, and Africa as the Hormuz crisis has choked off its key Middle Eastern deliveries, Malaysian Economy Minister Akmal Nasrullah Mohd Nasir said on Wednesday. headlines.title: UK Economy Set to Shrink as Iran Energy Shock Hits Growth
headlines.summary: The UK economy is expected to contract despite a boost at the start of the year, with economists warning that the war in Iran’s energy price shock will now show up in fresh data. headlines.title: EnQuest Bets $833 Million on Malaysia's Offshore Oil
headlines.summary: EnQuest PLC has agreed to buy interests in four production sharing contracts offshore Malaysia from local state firm Petronas for up to $833 million, as the UK-based oil and gas producer bets on growth opportunities in Southeast Asia.
